title: Join the lab
text:
Join the lab
Our lab is always interested in welcoming motivated people interested in:

Thermal and hydric biology
Physiological ecology

Evolutionary physiology

Quantitative synthesis (meta-analysis)

If you are interested in joining the lab (undergraduate, graduate, or postdoctoral), please get in touch via email with a brief description of your interests and background.



Mentoring phylosophy
Our approach to mentoring integrates physiology, ecology, and evolutionary biology through a quantitative and organismal perspective. We emphasise critical thinking, experimental design, data analysis, and writing, with the ultimate aim to equip students with the tools necessary to investigate biological questions across levels of organisation as well as efficiently communicate their research to a broader audience.
We are committed to training students at all levels, from undergraduate research projects to graduate supervision. We adopt an active-learning approach, combining conceptual and data-driven discussions.



Contact information
Dr. Danilo Giacometti | dgiacometti@usp.br
Laboratory of Integrative Physiology and Animal Performance (LaFIDA)
Department of Physiology, Institute of Biosciences
University of São Paulo São Paulo, Brasil

title: Research
text:
Research at LaFIDA focuses on how environmental conditions shape the physiology and behaviour of ectothermic animals. We are particularly interested in the integration of thermal biology, hydroregulation, energetics, and evolutionary physiology, and how these processes influence performance, ecological interactions, and responses to environmental change.


Thermal biology
We investigate how temperature influences physiological performance and behaviour. This includes the study of behavioural thermoregulation in natural and laboratory settings, thermal performance curves, and the functional consequences of thermal variability across temporal and spatial scales.



Hydroregulation
The maintenance of water balance is a critical challenge for many terrestrial ectotherms, particularly amphibians. Our research examines mechanisms of water loss and gain, and how hydration state constrains behaviour, physiology, and ecological performance.



Energetics and performance
We study how energy is acquired, allocated, and expended, and how these processes shape organismal performance. This includes linking metabolic processes to behaviour and ecological outcomes.



Evolutionary physiology
We investigate how physiological traits evolve in response to environmental pressures, with a particular focus on thermal and hydric challenges. By integrating comparative and experimental approaches, we aim to understand how variation in physiology arises across species and how it contributes to adaptation, performance, and ecological diversification.



Quantitative syntheses
We use quantitative synthesis approaches, including meta-analysis and comparative methods, to integrate findings across studies and species. These approaches allow us to identify general patterns, test broad hypotheses, and place experimental results within a wider ecological and evolutionary context.

title: Home
text:
Welcome to the Laboratory of Integrative Physiology and Animal Performance (LaFIDA). LaFIDA is based at the Department of Physiology, Institute of Biosciences, University of São Paulo, Brasil.
At LaFIDA, we investigate how environmental conditions shape the physiology and behaviour of animals. Our research focuses on the integration of thermal biology, water balance, and energetics within a comparative framework. While our research focuses primarily on ectothermic vertebrates such as amphibians and reptiles, we adopt Krogh’s principle and select study systems that best match the question being addressed.
By combining laboratory experiments, field studies, and quantitative syntheses, we aim to understand how organisms cope with environmental variation across time and space, and how these responses influence their performance in a changing world.

title: Code & Data
text: We develop and share code to support data analysis, visualisation, and reproducible research workflows. Our work emphasizes transparency, accessibility, and reusability across projects.
